properties.name: A1A Historic Byway
properties.description: Follows the Atlantic coast through historic Flagler Beach and the Matanzas Inlet area, connecting Spanish colonial heritage with coastal marshes and barrier island scenery.

properties.name: A1A Ocean Islands Trail
properties.description: Traces the northern Atlantic coast from Jacksonville Beach through Ponte Vedra and Amelia Island, passing sea turtle nesting beaches and salt marshes.

properties.name: Big Bend Scenic Byway
properties.description: One of Florida's most remote and rewarding drives, hugging the Gulf coast through Wakulla, Jefferson, Taylor, and Dixie counties where the Panhandle meets the Peninsula. Manatees, ospreys, and near-empty roads.

properties.name: Bradenton Beach Highway
properties.description: A short scenic corridor along Anna Maria Island's Gulf-facing shore, known for its Old Florida beach town character and calm turquoise waters.

properties.name: Broward County A1A Scenic Highway
properties.description: The Fort Lauderdale stretch of A1A runs between Deerfield Beach and Hollywood, mixing beachfront parks, marinas, and Art Deco-era commercial strips.

properties.name: Courtney Campbell Scenic Highway
properties.description: A 11-mile causeway crossing Old Tampa Bay between Tampa and Clearwater, with unobstructed water views in both directions and a dedicated recreational trail alongside.

properties.name: Florida Black Bear Scenic Byway
properties.description: Winds through the Ocala National Forest — the largest sand pine scrub forest in the world — with frequent wildlife sightings including the Florida black bear, deer, and sandhill cranes.

properties.name: Florida Keys Scenic Highway
properties.description: US-1 through the Florida Keys, crossing 42 bridges over open water from Key Largo to Key West. One of the most iconic drives in the United States.

properties.name: Green Mountain Scenic Byway
properties.description: Climbs through the Lake Wales Ridge in Lake County — Florida's most unusual terrain, with ancient sand scrub habitat, citrus groves, and some of the highest elevation in the state.

properties.name: Halifax Heritage Scenic Byway
properties.description: Follows the Halifax River through the Daytona Beach area, connecting Ormond Beach to Port Orange along the Intracoastal Waterway with a backdrop of old estates and riverfront parks.

properties.name: Heritage Crossroads Scenic Highway
properties.description: Crosses the agricultural heartland of Alachua and Columbia counties, passing through communities that preserve cracker Florida heritage, tobacco barns, and longleaf pine forests.

properties.name: Indian River Lagoon — Treasure Coast
properties.description: The southern extension of the Indian River Lagoon corridor through Martin County, where the lagoon narrows and deepens near the St. Lucie Inlet.

properties.name: Indian River Lagoon Scenic Highway
properties.description: Runs alongside the Indian River Lagoon in Brevard and Indian River counties — one of the most biodiverse estuaries in North America, with manatees, dolphins, and 600+ species of fish.

properties.name: JC Penney Memorial Highway
properties.description: Passes through the rural creek lands of Clay County near Penney Farms, an early 20th-century retirement community founded by the department store magnate, surrounded by cypress swamps and Black Creek.

properties.name: Lemon Bay / Myakka River Trail
properties.description: Explores the Charlotte County coast between Englewood and Venice, where Lemon Bay's mangrove-lined shores meet the Myakka River's freshwater corridor.

properties.name: Martin Grade Scenic Highway
properties.description: A rural corridor through Martin County's western agricultural lands, connecting the coast to the Everglades agricultural area through scrub flatwoods and cattle country.

properties.name: Old Florida Heritage Highway
properties.description: Traces SR-25 through Gilchrist and Alachua counties, where longleaf pine forests and sinkhole lakes characterize a landscape that looks much as it did a century ago.

properties.name: Ormond Loop and Trail
properties.description: A scenic loop north of Daytona Beach through Ormond Beach and the Tomoka Basin, passing the birthplace of Florida's auto racing heritage alongside moss-draped live oak hammocks.

properties.name: Palma Sola Scenic Highway
properties.description: A short but distinctive corridor along the Palma Sola Bay causeway in Manatee County, flanked by water on both sides and popular with birders for its shorebird and wading bird populations.

properties.name: Pensacola Bluffs Highway
properties.description: Follows the high bluffs above Escambia Bay in northwest Florida, offering panoramic views unusual for Florida along with Civil War-era fortifications and longleaf pine restoration areas.

properties.name: River of Lakes Heritage Corridor
properties.description: Follows the St. Johns River corridor through Volusia and Lake counties — a chain of shallow lakes and wetlands that form one of Florida's most important freshwater ecosystems.

properties.name: Scenic Highway 30-A
properties.description: Threads through the South Walton beach communities of Seaside, Rosemary Beach, and Grayton Beach — Florida's emerald coast, with rare coastal dune lakes found almost nowhere else on earth.

properties.name: Scenic Sumter Heritage Byway
properties.description: Winds through Sumter County's rolling hills and small towns, past The Villages and into the older agricultural landscape of Bushnell and Webster.

properties.name: Suncoast Scenic Parkway
properties.description: The northern approach to Tampa through Pasco and Hillsborough counties, with preserved wetland buffers and wildlife crossings that make it a model for Florida highway design.

properties.name: Tamiami Trail: Windows to the Gulf Coast
properties.description: The southwest Florida stretch of the historic Tamiami Trail through Charlotte and Lee counties, running through Charlotte Harbor — one of Florida's most productive estuaries for fishing and wildlife.

properties.name: The Ridge Scenic Highway
properties.description: Climbs the Lake Wales Ridge in Polk County, passing through citrus groves, ancient scrub preserves, and Bok Tower Gardens — one of Florida's most beautiful designed landscapes.

properties.name: William Bartram Scenic and Historic Highway
properties.description: Follows the path of 18th-century naturalist William Bartram through Putnam County along the St. Johns River, through the same forests and rivers he documented in his landmark 1791 travel narrative.
